mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing cycle that reduces discount rates for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as market participants anticipate lower borrowing costs.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods of FEDFUNDS decline are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC falls below 0.03 over 2-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp increase in the unemployment rate above 9.5% signals deteriorating labor market conditions, reducing corporate profit margins and investor confidence, which predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns remain positive in 60%+ of instances where UNRATE exceeds 9.5% or IC drops below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent declines in industrial production (INDPRO) and housing starts (HOUST) indicate weak supply-side dynamics, leading to lower corporate earnings growth and negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20d returns are positive in >50% of periods where both INDPRO and HOUST decline, or interaction effect size drops below 0.15 in rolling regressions.

mechanism_narrative: Rising residential sales (RSAFS) combined with declining housing starts (HOUST) signals a housing market correction, creating uncertainty in consumer spending and predicting negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS rises > 2% in 1mo while HOUST falls > 1% in 1mo
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns are positive in >40% of instances with conflicting RSAFS/HOUST signals or IC < 0.025.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent inflation above 215 in CPIAUCSL triggers central bank rate hikes, increasing discount rates and reducing equity valuations, which predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns are positive in 60%+ of CPIAUCSL >215 periods or IC < 0.015.

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll gains (PAYEMS) above 130k in a month signal robust labor market demand, boosting consumer spending and predicting positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are negative in 50%+ of PAYEMS >130k periods or IC < 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A rebound in GDP growth (GDPC1) after two consecutive quarterly declines signals economic recovery, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as investors price in stronger corporate earnings.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 turns positive after two consecutive negative prints
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns are negative in 50%+ of GDPC1 recovery periods or IC < 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production (INDPRO) combined with rising capacity utilization signals overheating supply chains, creating inflationary pressures that predict negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls > 1% in 1mo while CPIAUCSL rises > 0.5% in 1mo
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns are positive in >40% of INDPRO/CPIAUCSL divergent periods or interaction effect size < 0.1.
